title: Sunday Morning at Turkey Point
description:
Join Chris Marks and CMBO naturalists and walk New Jersey’s miracle mile for an exciting array of winter birds! We’ll explore the Glades Wildlife Refuge and Dividing Creek to enjoy wintering raptors, including lots of Bald Eagles, and wintering shorebirds, gulls and ducks, including Green-winged Teal, Bufflehead, Hooded Mergansers, Common Mergansers and Bonaparte’s Gulls. We may walk up to 1.5 miles, over generally level, sometimes muddy or sandy terrain.  
PREREGISTRATION REQUIRED – Ticket sales end 24hrs before start of program 



COST: $20 members, $25 nonmembers.  


Interested in becoming a member? Please click on the following link to learn more or join: Become a Member today! 


IMPORTANT INFORMATION:  
We bird in all weather. Be sure to check the forecast in advance and dress in layers appropriate for prevailing weather conditions. The weather in Cape May can differ considerably from nearby inland areas and may be warmer, colder, or windier so it’s best to be over prepared. We may have to bird in the rain, so please bring your rain gear. 


CANCELLATION: NJ Audubon’s CMBO programs are virtually never cancelled. If we cancel, due to lack of participation, severe weather, or unforeseen circumstances, all participants will be notified as soon as possible and are eligible for a full refund. Participants may cancel without penalty if greater than 48 hours in advance. Sorry, no refund for cancellations less than 48 hours in advance unless due to health emergencies; however, registrations can be transferred to another program in the future. 
REGISTER HERE
ics_categories: Cape May Bird Observatory's Northwood Center

title: Meditation, Movement, and The Muse
description:
Join The Writers Circle for an afternoon of yoga, meditation and creative writing! Writing may seem like a purely intellectual activity, but movement and meditation can play huge parts in the creative process. In this afternoon workshop, we’ll explore how mindfulness meditation, body awareness, and yoga can inspire and unblock the flow of your words. We will discover these tools through gentle guided meditation and movement activities, and explore how to apply them through focused writing prompts. 
The workshop includes an introduction to mindfulness meditation, a gentle 45-minute yoga class, and plenty of opportunities to write and share–if you choose! 
Whether you are working to develop a daily writing practice or to gain freer access to your literary imagination, this workshop will help you discover how your body can give you information about yourself and others, how to quiet the inner saboteur, and how a deeper awareness of the body can enhance your writing. 
Participants should bring their own yoga mats and writing materials (preferably notebooks and pens for writing by hand), and wear comfortable clothing suited for movement.

title: Special Screening: SPACE COWBOY
location: The Clairidge, 486 Bloomfield Ave, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
March 15 Only!
Joe Jennings, an innovator in the world of skydiving, has spent decades capturing breathtaking aerial moments with his camera for extreme sports competitions, Super Bowl commercials, and Hollywood blockbusters. Driven by a passion for “camera flying” in his youth, Joe cultivated a deep friendship with fellow skydiver Rob Harris, partnering to become one of the most influential skysurfing teams in history. Flying at the top of their game, Joe’s world was shattered when tragedy struck, leaving him to navigate the skies alone. Never giving up, he continued to push the boundaries of aerial cinematography and skydiving, finding solace in the clouds while grappling with profound grief and depression. Now, at 62, this pioneering skydiver is taking on a new challenge, elevating his art with a daring “flying car” stunt. Directors Marah Strauch (SUNSHINE SUPERMAN) and Bryce Leavitt delve into Joe’s extraordinary 40-year career in the sky. Through stunning archival footage, revealing interviews, and dramatic verité, they uncover a deeper emotional journey as Joe navigates loss, depression, and the challenge of balancing a life in the sky with the realities on the ground. 
Q&A with Subject Joe Jennings and Filmmakers Marah Strauch and Eric Bruggemann to follow

title: The Penny Power Project: Financial Literacy for Kids (Grades 2-5)
description: The Penny Power Project aims to teach the students about the essentials of financial literacy. Throughout the three-week course, the program will cover various topics like budgeting, saving, investing, loans, the history of money, the functions of money, exchange rates, and how interest works. Additionally, the course will also instruct students on how they can apply what they’ve learned to their lives through fun activities and videos.

description:
Ukrainian Contemporary Music Festival at Montclair State University
Inaugural Montclair State University presentation of the only festival in North America dedicated to showcasing Ukraine's complex and diverse contributions to contemporary music.
Concert fearturing Montclair State University Faculty, students and a special guest The Rhythm Method Quartet
The Rhythm Method is a quartet of composer-performers reimagining the string quartet through a contemporary, intersectional feminist lens—collaborating closely with composers, blending improvisation and notation, amplifying marginalized voices, and fostering collective creativity through performance and education.
All are welcome. Reservations are not necessary.

location: The Eclectic Chic Boutique, 547 Bloomfield Avenue, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Join us and learn basic sewing and design techniques to update your wardrobe to match your gender identity and style! Learning to sew is a great life skill to have, you can make your own repairs and adjustments, as well as repurpose old clothes to turn them into one of a kind fashion. No sewing experience necessary. Bring one piece of clothing you would like to upcycle. If you have a sewing machine or sewing needles and materials from home you want to use you are welcome to bring in and use during this session. We will have some sewing materials and tools on hand for you to use and get creative. Allies are also welcome to join. 
This event is in partnership with Out Montclair. Visit https://outmontclair.org/ to learn more about their mission.

title: Integrative Studio II Student-led Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design is delighted to announce the Integrative Studio II Student-Led Exhibitions for the Spring 2026 semester. The exhibition will be presented in Finley Gallery & the adjoining Annex Galleries from March 3rd through March 17th, with a public reception on March 3rd from 3:00pm until 4:30pm. These are the new rescheduled dates from the week prior due to snow. 
 
The ISII exhibition is the culmination of a six-week journey shared by visual arts majors across two sections of Integrative Studio II. Using the piñata as a point of origin for their investigation, the artists delved deeply into the subject to expand their understanding of its history, materiality, and cultural significance. Building on this initial research, they created artworks across media exploring a range of themes, including construction, deconstruction, regeneration, and transformation. The artists themselves collectively organized this public sharing of their work—together they met the challenge of presenting a visual through line within the shared gallery spaces while still highlighting the unique voices of each class, as reflected by the two exhibition subtitles: From the Outside In, and Divergence of Reactions. 
 
From the Outside In led by Professor Jess Blaustein
 
From the Outside In features diverse works of thirteen artists from Integrative Studio 2, providing an intimate glimpse into their personal memories, emotions, and cultural backgrounds. From larger than life to up close and personal, these exceptional works touch upon the wondrous human experience on a whole new level.
 
Students: Marley Balkau, Sandra Benlian, Emily Carrasco Perez, Ibelis Feliciano, Lisette Flor, Nila Freeman, Lena Harrison, Jenny Hernandez, Chloe Hwang, Brianna Mariconda, Hannah Michelson, Yanilda Munoz, Yan Bing Tan
 
Divergence of Reactions led by Professor Genevieve Lowe
 
Divergence of Reactions shares works made by a diverse group of growing artists. The artists cover a wide range of topics in their work, bravely sharing emotional themes such as anxiety, depression, and different forms of abuse. Additional elements the work addresses include beauty, sportsmanship, fragility of life and more - giving viewers a window into the artists personal experiences.
 
Students: Ofelia Alves, David Arrigoni, Anandria Campbell, Kalee Chaudhry, Kaitlyn Ferguson, Andrew Frazier, Keith Melendez, Bridget Perez, Stanley Piercin, Shyeann Rapley, Francois Rivero, Swetha Sivakumar, Sam Wortche
 
Integrative Studio II Student-led ExhibitionFinley Gallery, Finley HallExhibition dates: March 3 - March 17, 2025 (Gallery closed during Spring break from 3/7-3/15)Opening Reception: Tuesday, March 3, 2026, 3:00pm–4:30pm
 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12316901

Open Play is the perfect setting for players who know the basics and want to enjoy friendly games, practice their skills, and build confidence at the table. We'll bring the tiles, and all the accessories.  You bring your 2025 National Mah Jongg League card and your love of the game! The focus is on learning to play independently, building camaraderie, and having fun. Come meet fellow Mah Jongg enthusiasts, sharpen your strategies, and enjoy the lively spirit of the game!

Students must know the basics of mah jongg.  If you're new to the game, or don't feel comfortable sitting at a table and playing with others at a similar level, please register for a Beginner class by clicking here. Skip dates are Feb. 9th and 16th.

	Students should have taken Beginner Mah Jongg or have equivalent knowledge and experience. 


Students should bring the 2025 National Mah Jongg League card to Open Play.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Billy Hart | Jazz Drum Faculty Masterclass
description:
Don’t miss this rare opportunity to learn from one of the most influential voices in modern jazz drumming.
Legendary jazz drummer Billy Hart, known for his work with Herbie Hancock, Stan Getz, and countless others, leads a dynamic masterclass at the Cali School of Music.
Billy Hart is one of the most influential jazz drummers of his generation, celebrated for his versatility across straight-ahead, avant-garde, and popular styles. He has released 12 albums as a leader and appeared on more than 600 recordings, working with legends such as Herbie Hancock, Miles Davis, Wes Montgomery, Stan Getz, and Charles Lloyd. A founding member of Quest and a longtime member of the Cookers, Hart also leads his own acclaimed quartet. Born in Washington, D.C., he began performing as a teenager with Buck Hill and Shirley Horn before touring in the 1960s with Jimmy Smith, Wes Montgomery, and Eddie Harris. Since then, his career has spanned collaborations with both jazz icons and experimental innovators.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

title: Art Forum featuring Alex Paik
description:
After Spring Break, join us for the next Art Forum speaker in this engaging series.
 
Alex Paik, an artist, curator, writer, and community builder based in Los Angeles, will discuss his artistic practice and share insights into his latest work. This talk will be held virtually. 
 
Using cut and folded hand-colored paper, he explores reflected color, visual counterpoint, and repetition, informed by his classical music training and interest in polyphonic structures. His work has been shown nationally and internationally, including a wall installation featured at Art on Paper. Paik is Founder and Director of Tiger Strikes Asteroid, a non-profit network of artist-run spaces, curator of the Satellite Art Show in Miami, Chief Curator at Trestle Gallery, and serves on the Steering Committee at GYOPO, a collective of diasporic Korean cultural producers.
 
Art Forum is a speaker series featuring artists, designers, art historians, curators, and art critics from around the world who present their work and ideas in an open forum.
 
📅 Art Forum
Monday, 3/16/26  5:20pm-7pm
Virtual - https://montclair.zoom.us/j/95440833597?pwd=k0zYvA1eT7mqHwCdSWIvWX00r1WCXl.1

title: MILL @ MPL: Taste of Tai Chi
description:
Note: This is a class run through the MILL. Please register through their website here.

T'ai Chi Ch'uan is a 1000 year old art of mind-body health and energy cultivation. “A Taste of Tai Chi” introduces students to the 12-posture Yang-style Short Form of Master Bow Sim Mark and is designed for students of all ages and levels of physical ability. No prior experience with T'ai Chi is necessary. The health benefits of T'ai Chi include balance, flexibility, strength, immune-system support, and stress relief.

LEWIS FREEMAN studied the “Taste of T’ai Chi” form with Don Madison (Dragon T'ai Chi Ch'uan Association) and achieved the level of Assistant Teacher in July 2019. He previously studied the Yang-style short form at Long River T’aiChi Circle in Manhattan in the 1990s. When he's not practicing T’ai Chi, Lewis teaches at Fordham University as Senior Lecturer in Communication and Media Studies and also consults for businesses and organizations.

title: Integrative Studio II Student-led Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design is delighted to announce the Integrative Studio II Student-Led Exhibitions for the Spring 2026 semester. The exhibition will be presented in Finley Gallery & the adjoining Annex Galleries from March 3rd through March 17th, with a public reception on March 3rd from 3:00pm until 4:30pm. These are the new rescheduled dates from the week prior due to snow. 
 
The ISII exhibition is the culmination of a six-week journey shared by visual arts majors across two sections of Integrative Studio II. Using the piñata as a point of origin for their investigation, the artists delved deeply into the subject to expand their understanding of its history, materiality, and cultural significance. Building on this initial research, they created artworks across media exploring a range of themes, including construction, deconstruction, regeneration, and transformation. The artists themselves collectively organized this public sharing of their work—together they met the challenge of presenting a visual through line within the shared gallery spaces while still highlighting the unique voices of each class, as reflected by the two exhibition subtitles: From the Outside In, and Divergence of Reactions. 
 
From the Outside In led by Professor Jess Blaustein
 
From the Outside In features diverse works of thirteen artists from Integrative Studio 2, providing an intimate glimpse into their personal memories, emotions, and cultural backgrounds. From larger than life to up close and personal, these exceptional works touch upon the wondrous human experience on a whole new level.
 
Students: Marley Balkau, Sandra Benlian, Emily Carrasco Perez, Ibelis Feliciano, Lisette Flor, Nila Freeman, Lena Harrison, Jenny Hernandez, Chloe Hwang, Brianna Mariconda, Hannah Michelson, Yanilda Munoz, Yan Bing Tan
 
Divergence of Reactions led by Professor Genevieve Lowe
 
Divergence of Reactions shares works made by a diverse group of growing artists. The artists cover a wide range of topics in their work, bravely sharing emotional themes such as anxiety, depression, and different forms of abuse. Additional elements the work addresses include beauty, sportsmanship, fragility of life and more - giving viewers a window into the artists personal experiences.
 
Students: Ofelia Alves, David Arrigoni, Anandria Campbell, Kalee Chaudhry, Kaitlyn Ferguson, Andrew Frazier, Keith Melendez, Bridget Perez, Stanley Piercin, Shyeann Rapley, Francois Rivero, Swetha Sivakumar, Sam Wortche
 
Integrative Studio II Student-led ExhibitionFinley Gallery, Finley HallExhibition dates: March 3 - March 17, 2025 (Gallery closed during Spring break from 3/7-3/15)Opening Reception: Tuesday, March 3, 2026, 3:00pm–4:30pm
 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12316902

title: Bible Study (Guild Room)
description:
From Daytime Bible Study Leader Paul N:
During Daytime Bible Study, we discuss the Bible and books that analyze the Bible in depth. We also have fun and food. In 2024, we studied Mark and screened films Sabbath and 1946: The Mistranslation That Shifted A Culture. We read books about Holy Week, the Sabbath, The Sermon On The Mount, and Biblical women. All reference resources, translations, and interpretations are welcome. 
Current Book: “Who Stole My Bible? Reclaiming Scripture As A Handbook For Resisting Tyranny” by Rev. Jennifer Butler. 
Past Bible Study Books Have Included:
Sabbath As Resistance by Walter Brueggeman
Entering The Passion Of Jesus by Amy-Jill Levine
Jesus’ Alternative Plan: The Sermon On The Mount by Richard Rohr

title: Weekly Food Recovery Initiative
description:
Join our weekly Food Recovery events, where dedicated volunteers and members gather to make a difference in our community. Each week, participants help collect perfectly good leftover food from Gourmet Dining and transport it to Blanton. There, the food is repackaged into individual microwavable containers and stocked in the food pantry, providing meals for those in need throughout the week. 
To Participate: RSVP to attend the event each week. Meet in Blanton Hall Residence Hall Kitchenette in the Communal Area (Tell them you are working with Food Recovery), Sign in via Event Pass and your set!
Requirements:

Volunteers must be Food Safe Certified to participate.
Complete the certification by following the step-by-step instructions provided in this link.
Once certified, email your certification to foodrecoverynetwork@sgamsu.org to confirm your eligibility to participate.

Your involvement helps reduce food waste and insecurity while supporting individuals in our community who are in need. We look forward to your participation!*Please Note: This event end time is dependent on the amount of food given. You can estimate for max of hour and a half for recoveries.

Hosted by: EATS by Gourmet Dining, Food Recovery Network, Red Hawk Pantry

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12331652

title: Montclair Garden Club Talk: Introduction to Native Gardening
location: Montclair Bellevue Ave Branch - Classroom, Montclair Bellevue Ave Branch - First Floor, Montclair Bellevue Avenue Branch
description:
Introduction to Native Gardening: The importance and benefits of native plants and how using native plants in home gardens provides the environment with connected habitats for conservation. Comprehensive review of New Jersey native plant species including trees, shrubs and perennials and where to source them.

Lecture presented by Essex County Master Gardener, Karen Fricke:

Born on a farm in central Illinois, Karen Fricke was digging in the dirt before she could walk.
Through her years as a magazine editor, high school teacher, graduate student, stay-at-home
mom to four, and fiber artist, she always found her way outside to the garden. She became a
Master Gardener in Maryland in 2014, and worked at a plant nursery, where she developed and
ran their educational workshop program. Karen and her husband moved to New Jersey in 2022
to be closer to children and grandchildren, and she transferred to the Essex County Master
Gardeners program, where she lectures for their Speakers Program on the importance of
incorporating Native Plants in our home gardens.

title: Practical Spanish 2
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

In this class you will continue to build on the knowledge acquired in Practical Spanish 1 or Spanish 1A. You will expand your vocabulary and continue to practice and add to your repertoire of useful expressions.

Topics covered:


	Additional conversation/questions- ordering food/ shopping/making a reservation/airport/train station; asking and talk about likes and dislikes;  locating buildings or monuments
	Making polite requests
	Interrogative adjectives- Qué/Cómo/Cuándo/Dónde/Quién/Por qué
	Forming questions
	More ar/ir/er regular and irregular verbs
	Gustar/IOP
	Ask and tell time
	Possessive adjectives and different ways of showing possession (His book/John’s book)
	Weather
	Idiomatic expressions with Tener
	Contrast and apply the use of Ser/ Estar.
	Culture/Food/Holidays/Traditions
	All related vocabulary



Prerequisite: Completion of Practical Spanish 1, Spanish 1A or equivalent.

title: Writing: Method and Motivation
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

A workshop for anyone who wants to write, or wants to infuse their existing writing practice with new energy. In each class, we'll consider one element of fiction or creative non-fiction, such as point of view, narrative voice, and what makes a piece of writing riveting, or boring. Students can expect in-class writing prompts, reading aloud, and optional homework. Positive encouragement, rather than formal critiques,  will be featured, and inspiration and organization for writers will be discussed. Students should bring whatever they use to write to each class.

title: Advanced Woodworking
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

For those with previous woodworking experience, this class will allow students to learn more advanced techniques. Students will have the opportunity to start a new project or bring in a project from home that they would like to work on.

This course will cover:
- Advanced use of saws, drills, routers, kreg jigs, dowel jigs, biscuit jointers, table saw, router table
- Various types of joinery, fasteners, and finishing
- Different edge finishes on router table
- Carving tools
- Corner clamps, bar clamps

PREREQUISITE: Completion of Woodworking 101.

Materials fee is dependent on project and payable to instructor at the beginning of class.
Small Shelves / Spice Rack / Record Storage Box: $20-$25
Shelves / End Tables / Night Stands / Stools: $60-75
Coffee Table: $100

title: Awakening Awareness: Embracing This Moment
description:
The Mindful Stream
Feel free to join as we get still and practise presence within this moment. We will discuss and observe the obstacles that, seemingly, block us from being present and bring awareness to our unconsciousness in a safe, inclusive and affirming way.

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teaching and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous.

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys has been passionate about mindfulness since 2010 and since 2021 he has been organising free, weekly online meditation groups. For more information on his free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group, visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/).

description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

description:
Join our weekly Food Recovery events, where dedicated volunteers and members gather to make a difference in our community. Each week, participants help collect perfectly good leftover food from Gourmet Dining and transport it to Blanton. To Participate: RSVP to attend the event each week. Meet in Blanton Hall Residence Hall Kitchenette in the Communal Area (Tell them you are working with Food Recovery), Sign in via Event Pass and your set!
Requirements:

Volunteers must be Food Safe Certified to participate.
Complete the certification by following the step-by-step instructions provided in this link.
Once certified, email your certification to foodrecoverynetwork@sgamsu.org to confirm your eligibility to participate.

Your involvement helps reduce food waste and insecurity while supporting individuals in our community who are in need. We look forward to your participation!*Please Note: This event end time is dependent on the amount of food given. You can estimate for max of hour and a half for recoveries.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095719

title: Montclair Bee City Kick-Off
location: Montclair Fire House, 1 Pine St., Montclair, NJ, 07042
description:
Learn about Montclair’s new designation as a Bee City and how you can get involved!



Join us on March 18 at 7 pm at the Montclair Fire House, 1 Pine St., to learn how the NEEC, as Montclair’s official Bee City facilitator, will be working with the township and local organizations, including the Montclair Garden Club and the Native Plant Society of NJ, to make the town more bee- and pollinator-friendly. 
All are welcome to come, find out about local pollinator garden projects—from meadows to corner gardens to sidewalk planters—and explore simple ways to make your own space a haven for native bees, butterflies, and other pollinators. No space is too small! 
If your organization has an interest in local parks, ecological gardening, pollinators, or wildlife habitat and would like to take part in this initiative, please let us know. Contact Dave Wasmuth at MontclairBeeCityUSA@neearth.org. For more details about Bee City Montclair, visit our website. 
Register for the Bee City event.

title: Special Screening: THE WOMAN OF STARS AND MOUNTAINS
location: The Clairidge, 486 Bloomfield Ave, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Wednesday, March 18 Only!
Rita, an indigenous woman from Mexico, left her Tarahumara mountain community in northern Mexico and embarked on a journey to Kansas. There, she was involuntarily detained and confined in a psychiatric hospital for 12 years because the hospital authorities could not ascertain her identity, origins, or the language she spoke. Upon the discovery of the truth, Rita returned to her community and her niece, Juanita, who cared for her. THE WOMAN OF STARS AND MOUNTAINS presents a moving portrait of Rita and explores the multiple forms of racism and discrimination that indigenous women face. 
A panel discussion and Q&A with Santiago Esteinou (Writer and Producer), Francisco G. Gómez (Co-Founder and Music Director of Raíces Cultural Center), Nicole Wines (Co-Founder and Director of Programming, Communications and Technology of Raíces Cultural Center), moderated by Karin Jervert (Mad In the World Liaison from Mad In America Foundation) follows the screening. 
Presented in Partnership with MAD in America.

title: Lenten Study "The Tears of Things - Wednesday evenings March 11,18,25 @7:30pm on Zoom
description:
How do we live compassionately in a time of violence and despair? What can we do with our private disappointments and the anger that we feel in such an unjust world? These are the questions many of us carry on our hearts.  This Lent, we will gather to explore them with Franciscan friar,Richard Rohr, as he turns to the writings of the Hebrew prophets to offer us a path forward today.

The prophets' writings reflect the full spectrum of human maturity.  In almost every case their criticism of culture and institutions take them on a journey from anger to sadness and ultimately, to compassion. They confront evil and injustice in a way that acknowledges the wholeness of history, the interconnectedness of every living being, and the reality of divine and universal love.  In doing so, they set the stage for Jesus, who follows this identical pattern. 

This Lent, join Rev. Ann for three Zoom sessions to explore what this ancient wisdom has to teach us in our own time of outrage and heartbreak. We encourage you to read the book to prepare for this study.  You can order it online HERE or through your favorite bookstore. 

To join the class click the ZOOM LINK HERE

We look forward to taking this Lenten journey together.

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond.
All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. title: Silent Book Club Montclair
description:
At Silent Book Club® there's no assigned reading. SBC members gather in bars, cafés, bookstores, libraries, and online to read together in quiet camaraderie. All readers are welcome—ebooks or audiobooks, poetry or prose, fiction or non-fiction... it's BYOBook.


Did you love silent reading time in school? Are you more of an introvert that loves books, but doesn't want to jump into a discussion group? Are you looking to read more, but struggle to sit down and just DO IT? If you answered 'yes' to any of these, you might like Silent Book Club! Each month, we meet around Montclair and enjoy each other's company with a book of our own choosing.

Registration is required. Location TBD.

Please be advised this book club will be meeting at a public location and therefore cannot guarantee a quiet reading environment. This book club is best for readers who enjoy a coffeeshop ambiance (low chatter, music, outside noise, etc.) or those who prefer to read with headphones.

title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

title: Art Meets Jazz: Brandee Younger
location: Montclair Art Museum, 3 South Mountain Ave, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Art Meets Jazz returns for its eighth installment in this annual performance series. This year’s program features two-time Grammy–nominated harpist and composer Brandee Younger, whose work continues to expand the role of the harp in contemporary jazz and popular music. 
Her latest release, Gadabout Season, is her third album for the legendary Impulse! label. Younger is an NAACP Image Award winner and a recipient of the 2025 Doris Duke Artist Award, the largest prize in the United States dedicated to individual artists. Her collaborations span genres and generations, including work with Pharoah Sanders, Ravi Coltrane, Christian McBride, John Legend, The Roots, and Beyoncé. 
Your ticket includes a wine and food reception beginning at 6 p.m., with catering provided by Montclair’s own MOSO Signature. 
Art Meets Jazz is presented by the Montclair Art Museum’s African American Cultural Committee (AACC). 
Must be 21+ to attend. 

Sponsorship:

$2,000–Celestial Sponsor

Private, center orchestra table seating for 4, meet-and-greet opportunity with the artist, special Art Meets Jazz event gift bag, and more


$1,200–Angel Sponsor

Private orchestra table seating for 4, meet-and-greet opportunity with the artist, and more


$600–Harp Sponsor

Premium orchestra seating for 2, meet-and-greet opportunity with the artist, and more

Premium orchestra seating for 1, meet and greet opportunity with the artist, and more



For more info on sponsorship benefits click here.

title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'
For more information

title: Loopwell x Merrill Lynch: Financial Well-Being — Navigating Life’s Transitions
location: Loopwell, 80 Maple Ave, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Life’s transitions — whether retirement, divorce, empty nesting, career shifts, or loss — can feel overwhelming, especially when financial decisions feel heavy and uncertain. Join us for a thoughtful, human-centered conversation exploring financial wellbeing as a deeply personal part of life and identity. This isn’t a lecture or sales pitch — it’s a space for honest dialogue, reflection, connection, and grounding as you navigate meaningful life chapters.

title: Food Security Food Panel Discussion
description:
Join us for a special edition of Temple Talks. Anne Merin, Executive Director of Toni’s Kitchen will share how you can become an ally in supporting your friends and neighbors to achieve Food Security.  State Senator John McKeon will discuss the importance of Public Private partnerships and the role of Government to support this important issue.  The Evening will be Moderated by Jim Axelrod of CBS News. The event will be held in person at Temple Ner Tamid, 936 Broad Street, Bloomfield, NJ 07003. CLICK HERE TO REGISTER! 
The event is free and open to the public. There will be a live steam option on the TNT Youtube Channel. There is no need to register if you plan to live stream. 
The Temple Talks Series at Temple Ner Tamid is hosted by Rabbi Marc Katz and moderated by a nationally known journalist. The objective of the series is to engage our audience with discussions by current leaders on current issues that impact all communities.

title: Panel Discussion on Food Insecurity with Anne Merin
description:
Join us on Thursday, March 19th at 7:30pm for a special edition of Temple Talks. Anne Merin, Executive Director of Toni’s Kitchen, will share how you can become an ally in supporting your friends and neighbors to achieve food security. The Panel will include State Senator John McKeon and Kathleen Smith of Partners for Health Foundation. Topics include the current crisis in Federal funding and the importance of public-private partnerships to achieve Food Security. The evening will be moderated by Jim Axelrod of CBS News. 
REGISTER HERE! 
The event is free and open to the public. This program will be available for streaming on the TNT YouTube channel.
There is no need to register if you plan to live stream. 
The Temple Talks Series at Temple Ner Tamid is hosted by Rabbi Marc Katz and moderated by a nationally known journalist.
The objective of the series is to engage our audiencewith discussions by current leaders on current issues that impact all communities.

title: Cru Large Group!
description:
Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ! Join us in learning together on Who Christ is, building a personal relationship with Him, and joining a community filled with love and open arms! 
This semester, we will be focusing on the theme "Identity in Christ." We pray that our students will learn more about who they are in Christ, and look at some examples of people in the Bible where God revealed their true identity in Christ!
"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved." - Acts 2:42-47

title: 2nd Annual STEM Summit: Building Skills for Success
location: Center for Environmental and Life Sciences, Atrium
description:
The 2nd Annual STEM Summit at Montclair State University will bring together students, faculty and industry professionals from across New Jersey, to explore the interdisciplinary skills shaping the future of science and mathematics. The summit will feature a keynote address and include student-led research presentations across diverse scientific disciplines.
 
Attendees will participate in engaging panels, networking opportunities and interactive workshops designed to enhance key professional skills. Through exploring diverse career opportunities in teaching, academia, industry, and beyond, participants will uncover how collaboration across disciplines drives innovation, problem-solving and real-world impact. 
 
The Summit aims to foster community and amplify all student voices in STEM by empowering the next generation of scientists and innovators.

Hosted by: College of Science and Mathematics (CSAM)

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12322030

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSiC |  Masterclass with Kyle Resnick
description:
Masterclass with Kyle Resnick
Free and Open to the Public, Reservations not Required
Join the brass area for a very special masterclass with trumpeter Kyle Resnick. Kyle will work with Cali students and share what he's learned on his incredible musical journey that has seen him performing and recording with Taylor Swift, Ed Sheeran, Bruce Springsteen, Beirut, The National, and Mumford and Sons, among others!
Bio:
Kyle Resnick is a trumpet player known for his versatility and contributions to both live performances and studio recordings across a wide variety of genres and musical styles. He has established himself as a prominent figure in the music industry through collaborations with a diverse array of artists and groups, from recording for Taylor Swift (“Folklore,” “Evermore,” and “Midnights”), Ed Sheeran and Bruce Springsteen, among several others, to Classical chamber and solo and countless orchestral performances on concert stages over three decades. He has felt equally at home on stages like Carnegie Hall, Lincoln Center, the Barbican, Royal Albert Hall, Sydney Opera House and venues such as the Hollywood Bowl, and arenas and stadiums around the globe. Kyle is a longstanding touring and recording member of the bands The National and Beirut, and has commenced in 2025 on a world tour with the band Mumford & Sons, for which he has also recorded on the last two albums. Since his time as a student to the present day, Kyle has found great satisfaction enriching his musical journey through teaching trumpet and directing ensembles, having served on the faculties of Juilliard Pre-College, Brooklyn College, SUNY University at Buffalo and SUNY Buffalo State.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12228722

title: Be Internet Awesome: Internet Safety For Kids (2nd-5th Graders)
location: Montclair Main - Storytime Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
In today's digital landscape, children need more than just access -- they need guidance, values, and tools to navigate it responsibly. That's where Be Internet Awesome comes in! In this program series, they'll learn the five Pillars: Smart, Alert, Strong, Kind, and Brave. These Pillars teach kids to think critically, protect their privacy, stand up to cyberbullying, and build a culture of respect and empathy online. 

Parents are encouraged to join our sessions. We will have some take-home materials available after each session to continue the learning at home! 

This session will cover the pillar: BRAVE

What the kids will learn: Kids consider who they can turn to when something online feels confusing or wrong. They're reminded that brave doesn't mean going it alone.

title: Hands-On Learning Takes Center Stage at the Montclair Public Library Auditorium: “Keyboard Fidgets” Workshop for NJ Makers Day
location: Montclair Public Library, 50 South Fullerton Avenue, Montclair, New Jersey, 07042, United States
description:
The Montclair Public Library will join libraries and educational centers statewide in celebrating NJ Makers Day, a three-day event highlighting creativity, innovation, and hands-on learning across New Jersey. This annual initiative invites residents of all ages to explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math (STEAM) through interactive projects and programs. 
As part of this year’s celebration, the library will host “Keyboard Fidgets”, a program designed for tweens and teens to build a fun, hands-on craft while learning basic design and assembly concepts. The program is sponsored by The Tutor Shack, a Montclair based tutoring center dedicated to fostering curiosity and confidence in learners of all ages. 
“Maker education nurtures problem-solving, creativity, and collaboration. Skills that are essential for lifelong learning,” said Steph Legnosky, owner of The Tutor Shack. “We’re proud to support NJ Makers Day because it sparks imagination and empowers students to see themselves as creators and innovators. Partnering with the Montclair Public Library for events like this aligns perfectly with our mission to strengthen the foundations of learning through curiosity and exploration.” 
The Keyboard Fidgets program will take place at the Montclair Public Library during NJ Makers Day weekend on Friday, March 20 from 4:00 – 5:30 PM. Make your very own keyboard fidget keychain with amazing teen library volunteers! For ages 8 and up. Drop-in; while supplies last.

description:
Erev Shabbat services begin on Friday at 6:30pm. These services are especially musical, often with a band of congregants accompanying the Cantor. Usually around an hour, these services are filled with a lot of spirit. It’s not uncommon to see people dancing in their seat or hear the whole congregation clapping their hands. Friday night is intergenerational. Glance around and see senior citizens sitting beside religious school families both getting something out of the services.  
In the summer months, services are led by members of the congregation with support from our clergy.
Join us most weeks from anywhere for Erev Shabbat Services through Zoom or our YouTube Livestream.

title: Worship Night (Campus Invasion)
location: Student Center 250BC - Ballrooms B and C
description:
Campus Invasion is a night set apart to lift up the name of Jesus at Montclair State University. On March 20th, we gather not just for music, but for worship—creating space for God’s presence to move freely among us.
This night is an invitation to pause from the busyness of campus life, lay down distractions, and come before God with open hearts. We believe worship is more than a song; it is surrender, alignment, and response to who God is. As we worship together, we are trusting God to refresh weary hearts, restore hope, and awaken a deeper hunger for Him.
Campus Invasion is about unity—students, friends, and the campus community coming together to seek God, pray, and declare Jesus as Lord over our lives and our campus. Whether you’ve been walking with God for years or you’re simply curious and searching, this night is for you.
Come expectant. Come ready to worship. Come as you are.Jesus will be glorified.

Hosted by: I lift Jesus

title: Billiards
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
***Meeting Point:***
Outside of the building; After 5-10 minutes: You can meet us upstairs at the pool tables. *\*\*Please look for the group as the staff do not keep up with the group\*\***
*** Price:
The price is $6.50 per hour per person if you have 4 players at your table. However, the final price is based on the total amount of minutes played. At the end of the hour, the table amount will be split per person. Each table should have a table leader, **this person will collect the money of players at their table and will pay for the table on one card.** 3-4 table leaders will be needed, **volunteering for this is encouraged**

***Bringing cash is highly encouraged to pay your portion to your table leader***

*Parking*: street parking is available however it may be hard to find close to the venue. There are side streets before the town proper, but a good option is the nearby ANT bookstore which has a lot which should be okay to use.

***This location has food & drinks available for purchase.***

*** Expectations**:
Pool cues are available at the location, expect mid to low lighting. If you come after 7:15pm, look for the Meetup group upstairs.
** *What if I'm a beginner or only want to play with people at a similar level?**
Tables will be divided between beginners, intermediate players, and advanced. However, this depends on how many types of players come, so it will be divided as best as possibly available. If you would like game rules, please speak with Marc.** Marc is also available to answer questions including how to hold the cue, stances, etc.**
*** Is anyone at Clifton Billiards after 9pm?
After 9pm, most go home or go to a local bar. If you'd like to hang out after, definitely speak with members to see if they want to do something after.
*** Can I switch tables?**
*Yes, but it does make it easier if you stay at your table. If you switch tables, pay the amount of time you were at that table. Example, if you were at able A for an hour, pay the hour to the table A leader. If you go table B for two hours, pay your two hours to the table B leader. At the end, the table leader is taking the payments for the total amount of time at that table.*
*** How do I get balls for the table and how many people do I tell staff that I have?*
*One person per table gives their ID to the staff in order to check out balls. Again, the price for 4 people is about $6.50. If you have more or less, it slightly changes. So it just depends on how many people are at your table. It may be better to mention that you have more people esp if you are starting with 4 or 5 people.*
*** I feel like people aren't trying to be my friend.*
*Please remember that different people have different goals. Some may come to just play billiards, others may be there to meet new people or do something social, and some may be there to make long term friends. Find people that match your goals or be ok with others that may not.
source: Meetup North Jersey Bowling Soccer Volleyball

title: First Mountain Ramble w/ Sharon ~6 miles
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Saturday March 21st 8:00-11:00. First Mountain Ramble. 5-6 miles on trails depending on trail condition. Moderate pace and difficulty.

**Meet**: Locust Grove parking lot.

**Leader**: Sharon Salmon 973-715-5554.

\*\*\* In registering for this or any other South Mountain Conservancy (SMC) programs in 2026 online, I hereby release and agree to hold the SMC, its volunteers, staff and Board of Trustees, harmless from and waive on behalf of myself, my heirs, and any personal representatives any and all causes of action, claims, demands, damages, costs, expenses and compensation for damage or loss to myself and/or property that may be caused by any act, or failure to act, in this SMC activity, or that may otherwise arise in any way in connection with any other SMC activity. I understand that this release discharges the SMC from any liability or claim that I, my heirs, or any personal representatives, may have against the SMC with respect to any bodily injury, illness, death, medical treatment, or property damage that may arise from, or in connection to, any SMC program. This liability waiver and release extends to the SMC along with Essex County and its staff and any other affiliated organization or SMC partner. I also understand that by signing below I approve the possible use of photos of me at this SMC event on social media or in promotional materials.
**BY REGISTERING FOR THIS HIKE I attest I am in good health and attest that I: Am not experiencing any symptoms of illness such as cough, shortness of breath or difficulty breathing, fever, chills, repeated shaking with chills, muscle pain, headache, sore throat, or new loss of taste or smell.**
**Am following all CDC recommended guidelines, above, and limiting my exposure to the novel coronavirus/COVID-19.**
\# Arrival\, Weather\, & Cancellations
Please come to the starting point 10 minutes ahead of time so the hike can start promptly. Times are our best estimates; they can vary depending on conditions, and the size and pace of a group. If you have any questions, call the leader. **Temperatures above 90 degrees and steady rain cancels, but intermittent drizzle does not. You will *not* be notified.** **Bring water. Wear hiking boots, preferably waterproof with good soles and traction devices and bring poles (optional).** Dogs are not allowed on hikes unless specified.
These hikes are publicized first to SMC members and then advertised to the public one week before a hike. There is a limit of 25 participants, so pre-registration is required at least two days before a hike to sign the liability waiver online and allow lists to be sent to hike leaders. If you have registered and then find you cannot attend, please cancel your order online. **Repeated no shows without prior cancellation will result in removal from list.**
**To become a current member visit [[https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)]([https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)).**

title: Metrotrails 29 Year Anniversary Hike: Warren County!
description:
Metrotrails: NJ, PA, NY Metropolitan Area Hiking and More!
Join us for our annual hike in Warren County, marking 29 years since we began doing these hikes.
I was still in high school for the first one, and we always did the old Warren Railroad.
Over the years, some properties became inaccessible, unsafe, or badly overgrown forcing us to change the route we take. In some ways, it's a good thing to cover some new ground, and in others we miss some places we love.
This time, we'll be following some of the things we've done as well as some new stuff we've never incorporated into this hike previously.
We'll meet before 9 at the Port Colden Mall, and then shuttle with as few vehicles as possible to our start point.
This time, we'll be out in the Pequest area for the beginning.
Several field trails make for a very pleasant way of starting.
We'll make our way casually through all of these woods, and then come to a short road walk in Townsbury to the Lehigh and Hudson River Railway grade.
We'll follow the L&HR west through Pequest and Buttzville, then make our way on road briefly to Hot Dog Johnny's for a probably late lunch.
Once finished at Johnny's, we'll head up to the old Lackawanna/Warren Railroad in the way we always walk back to Oxford.
When we get to Oxford, we'll make refreshment stops, and then head to the south. Rather than stay on the overgrown railroad bed, we'll likely follow some back roads including Tunnel Hill Road through farm land where we'll see some different railroad remnants, then Jackson Valley Road back to 31. We'll finish our evening at the newly reopened Draught House to conclude the evening.
If needed, contact me at 908 343 8374

title: Spring Birding Class - Migration
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
FREE Spring Birding Classes – Adults 
Explore the world of birds in the beautiful gardens of Van Vleck. Each class will focus on a specific topic to help us learn more about these amazing creatures. Weather permitting, a birding walk will be included so bring your binoculars (or borrow a pair from Van Vleck) and join us for some birding fun. Instructor: Naturalist Jenny Aley, Van Vleck Education Volunteer. 
March 21st – 9:00 AM -10:00 AM – Migration – Learn about migration and look for birds who are just passing through. 
Open to adults. Space is limited – register early.   
Free – Registration required – no drop-ins for this class. 
 
May 12th  – 9:00-10:00 AM – Birds and their Nests – Examine various bird nests and look at how birds prepare for their new families. 
June 27th – 9:00-10:00 AM – Young Birds – Sharpen your identification skills by focusing on young birds who don’t look exactly like adults yet.

title: Women's Breakfast (Lambert Castle)
description:
Join us to visit the historic Lambert Castle  
The Women of Union will be taking a tour of the amazing, newly restored Lambert Castle located at 3 Valley Road in Paterson. 
The tour will take place on Saturday, March 21 at 10:00 am at the Lambert Castle. 
“The Castle,” a well-known landmark in Northern New Jersey, is located on Garret Mountain overlooking the City of Paterson. Once a private residence, the building is fashioned after an English castle.  Catholina Lambert, an English immigrant who made his fortune in the silk industry, built the Castle in 1892-93.   He named it “Belle Vista.” 
We will travel to a diner for lunch after the tour. 
We will meet at church at 9:15 am to carpool to the castle. (This will take place of the monthly Women’s Breakfast.) 
Please email Karen Ermler at karen.ermler@gmail.com so we can let the tour guide know how many will be attending. 
This is an awesome historic opportunity!

title: [Outside Org]  Montclair Alumnae Chapter-Delta Sigma Theta
Balsam Lake Mountain Hike
description:
Take in classic Catskill views on this scenic hike to Balsam Lake Mountain via the Dry Brook Ridge Trail in the Balsam Lake Mountain Wild Forest. This moderately rated 6-mile out-and-back hike features a steady climb with 1,197 feet of elevation gain, winding through hardwood forests before reaching the summit. At the top, hikers are rewarded with sweeping mountain views and a historic fire tower that offers a perfect spot to rest, refuel, and soak it all in. This trail is ideal for hikers looking for a solid challenge with a big payoff.
How do I sign up?

Register in the Student Recreation Center main office or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students $10 / Faculty, Staff $20

Where and when?

Saturday, March 21, 2026
10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.
Begins at the Student Recreation Center

What to bring:Water, lunch/snacks, sunscreen, bug spray, layered clothing (depending on weather), sturdy close-toe hiking shoes or boots
Potential Hazards:Uneven and rocky terrain; steep sections; slippery surfaces (especially if wet); sudden weather changes; sun exposure; dehydration; insect bites; poisonous plants/allergies; wildlife encounters; and injuries including sprains/strains, abrasions, lacerations, and fractures.

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12265374

title: Outdoor Adventures: Spring Has Sprung
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
March 21st – Spring has Sprung 
Saturday, 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Join us as we celebrate the spring equinox at our March Outdoor Adventures program. Together we will explore the changes this season brings, what plants are starting to bloom in the gardens, and what wildlife we find in the springtime.   
On our Outdoor Adventures, you and your family explore and learn about the outdoor world through crafts, scavenger hunts, activity sheets and educational displays. Each program will focus on a specific theme, and our Van Vleck Education team will guide your family through this interactive learning experience. This program is entirely outdoors, weather dependent. Registration is required and includes everything you need to participate in the program. Designed for children ages 4 -11 but all are welcome. 
Fee: FREE for Members of Van Vleck; $15 per family  for  General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class 
 
Sponsored by  
  
Upcoming Outdoor Adventures 
April 11th – Arbor Day Everyday 
Arbor Day is a day to reflect on the importance of trees in our environment. We have an abundance of beautiful trees throughout the gardens. At April’s Outdoor Adventures, learn more about our trees as we explore their role in our environment, their unique leaf shapes, and what animals use trees as their homes.  
  
May 16th – Honeybees & Hummingbirds 
What do bees and hummingbirds have in common? They fly and they are pollinators! Join us to learn about your local pollinators, how they make a positive impact on our environment, and what we can do to help them on their mission.  
  
June 6th – Insect Safari  
You can find many different insects buzzing, wiggling, and crawling around the gardens. Join us for an Insect Safari at this month’s Outdoor Adventures. Create a bug inspired craft, take a hike looking for insects, and learn about the important roles insects play in the garden.

title: Walk at Grover Cleveland Park
description:
Let's Walk!!
We will meet near the fishing pond entrance of the park, close to Brookside Avenue and the skating house/restrooms.
**GPS location: 138 Brookside Ave, Caldwell. Park on the street.**

Grover Cleveland Park in Caldwell/Essex Fells celebrated its centennial in 2013. As the first county park in western Essex County, the site was originally selected for its natural beauty and its potential to provide a space for relaxation and recreation. It contains gentle rolling hills, broad meadows and other environmentally aesthetic features

We will walk for about an hour or at least 3 miles.

**Please arrive 5 minutes early, so we can start on time.**

description:
Join us for a night written in the stars!
Tadhana, the Filipino word for destiny and fate, is the unseen force that guides our stories, even when we don’t realize it. MUFASA invites you to step into that magic at our annual cultural ball inspired by precolonial Filipino mythology.
Come dressed as a deity, monster, or myth that speaks to you. This is a night for self-expression, creativity, and honoring our heritage while choosing how we show up in the present.

title: An Evening With Josh Joplin and Jeffrey Gaines in Concert
location: First Congregational Church of Montclair, 40 S. Fullerton Avenue, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Josh Joplin isn’t one to dwell on past glories, but there was a time when he was chasing songs worldwide. In 2001, his breakout single “Camera One” became the first independently released song to hit #1 at Triple A Top 40, landing Josh Joplin Group on Artemis Records—a label that boasted acts as varied as J. Mascis, Warren Zevon, The Pretenders, and, yes, the “Who Let the Dogs Out” hitmakers, The Baha Men. (“Camera One” may not have unleashed any canines, but it did land a spot on the cult classic tv show Scrubs.) 
Joplin followed with The Future That Was (2002), produced by Rob Gal (Magnapop, Rock-a-Teens), and later earned critical praise for Jaywalker, Among the Oak & Ash, and Devil Ship. Now, after years of collaborating with friends and fellow musicians, he returns with his latest album, GpYr (“gap year”). Spin Magazine says GpYr is “a collection of pop-infused gems that, with help of producer Lorenzo Wolff (Taylor Swift’s Midnights and Folklore), captures the raw energy of Joplin’s earlier works…but with the musical and lyrical layers of the older, wiser musician Joplin has become.” More than just a record, GpYr is a sonic scrapbook—a story of four friends who, over one unforgettable summer and autumn, came together for a mission: to rescue Seely from a therapeutic boarding facility in remote New England. In saving her, they saved each other.While the Josh Joplin Group will celebrate the 25th anniversary of Useful Music—the album some say started it all–it’s Joplin’s “new album that reminds us all of how far we’ve come.” (Spin Magazine) 
Jeffrey Gaines
Jeffrey Gaines has been heralded for his soul-searching lyrics and his powerful live performances. With only his voice and a guitar for accompaniment, Gaines has earned a reputation as a captivating performer, entertaining his audiences worldwide. 
Throughout his three-decade recording career, Gaines has maintained an impressive standard for soul-searching, introspective lyrics and catchy, uplifting melodies. Since bursting on the scene with his self-titled 1992 debut album, the charismatic singer-songwriter-guitarist has built a beloved body of recordings that’s won him a large and deeply devoted international fan base. He has sold out theaters and clubs throughout North America and Europe. With his compelling live performances gaining high respect, Gaines was asked to join tours as support act for Tom Petty & The Heartbreakers in Europe, Tracy Chapman, and Stevie Nicks with Sheryl Crow, among others. 
Gaines landed his first record deal shortly after graduating from high school, and in 1992 released his widely acclaimed debut album Jeffrey Gaines. The eponymous disc won massive amounts of critical and audience acclaim, spawning the hit “Hero in Me.” Somewhat Slightly Dazed (1994) and Galore (1998) further expanded his audience. Interview magazine described Gaines’ work as “soul-searching” and “refreshingly free of jargon, sentiment or cliché.” 
In 2001, Gaines’ release Always Be yielded a surprise hit in his deeply felt interpretation of Peter Gabriel’s “In Your Eyes”; 2003’s Toward the Sun attracted even more critical accolades, with Mojo praising the “soul-folkie’s sexy growl” and The Boston Globe declaring that “Gaines’ soulful, wounded vocals make a case for his being one of pop’s finest singers.” Toward the Sun was followed by a pair of live albums, the 2004 CD/DVD Jeffrey Gaines Live and 2012’s Live in Europe, which reflected the deep and abiding rapport that Gaines has with his fans. In 2018, Gaines released his eighth studio album, Alright. The album was recorded in Los Angeles with Chris Price, Val McCallum and Elvis Costello’s Imposters, Pete Thomas and Davey Faragher. 
“The biggest pleasure I derive from this is playing live,” Gaines notes. “There’s nothing more satisfying for me than a good live gig. I’m in control, but the audience can take it to a place that’s higher than I can imagine. When I show up for a live gig, I don’t need much, there’s no rider. It’s just, turn it on, let the audience in, and we’ll do the rest. It’s the best job anyone could ever have.” 
With more than a quarter-century of recording and performing under his belt, Jeffrey Gaines is enjoying life and music more than ever. “Nowadays, I know what the job is about so there’s nothing to stress about,” he says. “I know that there’s people out there who want it, so I have the opportunity to extend their experience. I guess I could lay my albums all out and see that they tell a story, but personally I prefer to keep looking forward.”

description:

The NJS gave its very first concert right here in Montclair and has since evolved into a nationally recognized orchestra known for its artistic excellence and deep community engagement. 
This concert will feature a woodwind trio: 
   
Bart Feller, flute                   Bob Ingliss, oboe           Juan Esteban Martinez, clarinet

description:

Dr. Spiegel’s innovative seders have been featured in The New York Times and on PBS, and this intriguing, humorous, multimedia presentation has inspired unforgettable seders around the world. 
In this workshop you will discover music, games, parodies, and quizzes, historic and global Jewish cultural connections, and how Shakespeare, Donald Duck, Klingons, lawyers, Phoenicians, and Jews around the world “ask” the Four Questions.

title: Pride Choir Rehearsal (Sanctuary and Assembly Room)
Special Screening: TOKYO STORY (1953)
location: The Clairidge, 486 Bloomfield Ave, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
A profoundly stirring evocation of elemental humanity and universal heartbreak, TOKYO STORY is the crowning achievement of the unparalleled Yasujiro Ozu. The film, which follows an aging couple’s journey to visit their grown children in bustling postwar Tokyo, surveys the rich and complex world of family life with the director’s customary delicacy and incisive perspective on social mores. Featuring lovely performances from Ozu regulars Chishu Ryu and Setsuko Hara, TOKYO STORY plumbs and deepens the director’s recurring theme of generational conflict, creating what is without question one of cinema’s mightiest masterpieces.
This special collaboration between Montclair Film and La Cinémathèque celebrates Lives Remembered: Japanese Cinematic Masterpieces. Featuring a special introduction and Q&A with curator, Gerard Amsellem, local teacher, artist, and filmmaker from New Wave Productions along with Dr. Anne Giblin Gedacht, Ph.D., Associate Professor at Seton Hall University on Japanese culture.
Presented in Partnership with La Cinémathèque Film Club and New Wave Productions.

title: Women Impressionist Painters: Mary Cassatt and Berthe Morisot NEW - Virtual Program
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

​Despite their relative obscurity, many women painters reached impressive heights of creative achievement and professional success in the 19th to the early 20th century in Paris. Two of the most exceptional are Mary Cassatt and Berthe Morisot. These women defied many social norms of their time to join the avant-garde alongside their more- famous counterparts, Manet, Monet, Renoir, Degas, and Pissarro. Exploring themes of modernity, they painted imaginative portraits, bourgeois family and leisure life, unconventional landscapes, and contemporary fashion. Come and hear about their struggles and triumphs and see an array of their astonishing and innovative works.

title: Mah Jongg: Open Play
location: Montclair Main - Cafe Area, Montclair Public Library
description:
PLEASE NOTE: You must sign up for EACH INDIVIDUAL SESSION. Signing up for one session does not register you for any following classes. Open Play is open on a first come, first served basis. 

Open Play is the perfect setting for players who know the basics and want to enjoy friendly games, practice their skills, and build confidence at the table. We'll bring the tiles, and all the accessories.  You bring your 2025 National Mah Jongg League card and your love of the game! The focus is on learning to play independently, building camaraderie, and having fun. Come meet fellow Mah Jongg enthusiasts, sharpen your strategies, and enjoy the lively spirit of the game!

Students must know the basics of mah jongg.  If you're new to the game, or don't feel comfortable sitting at a table and playing with others at a similar level, please register for a Beginner class by clicking here. Skip dates are Feb. 9th and 16th.

 

PLEASE NOTE:


	Students should have taken Beginner Mah Jongg or have equivalent knowledge and experience. 


Students should bring the 2025 National Mah Jongg League card to Open Play.

location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Journaling in the Garden 
Monthly on Mondays, 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM. (Series or individual Classes) 
March 23rd 
April 13th 
May 11th 
June 15th 
Explore the beauty of the Van Vleck Gardens this spring through reflective writing and creative mark-making.  This mindfulness-based class/series invites you to slow down, observe the natural world and turn your blank pages into a wonderland of personal observations.  Each class takes place in a different area of the Gardens (weather permitting), offering new inspiration for discovery.  Deepen your connection with nature and yourself in a no-pressure environment.  No art or writing experience necessary – just an openness to explore, reflect and create.   
Fee: Series Rate (4 classes): $50 Members / $70 General Public 
Individual Rate: $15 Members / $20 General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to class.

title: Mushuk Nina - Fuego Nuevo -Expressing the Art and Language of the Andes
description:
The name Mushuk Nina means New Fire. In Andean cosmology, fire is one of the four important elements that also include air, earth, and water. The Mushuk Nina symbolizes the renewal of our energies, is a Kichwa phrase meaning "New Year" representing the Andean New Year and a festival of renewal celebrated around the March equinox (typically March 21). It symbolizes the start of a new agricultural and spiritual cycle, focusing on purification, community gathering, and gratitude to Pachamama (Mother Earth). 
In this event, Kichwa community leader, Atik Paguay, and acclaimed artist Raul Ayala, will lead an artistic and cultural event that will bring us from learning about this celebration and its meaning to participate to artistic creation led by Raul Ayala, to the construction of the "churu" (Snail) in the ground with music and stomping ceremony.
Join us!

Hosted by: Center for Latino Heritage & Spanish Language Excellence, Spanish and Latino Studies

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12316898

title: GOVERNING BEYOND ELECTIONS: ACCOUNTABILITY, PROSPERITY AND THE AMERICAN ADMINISTRATIVE STATE
location: Presentation Hall (College of Communication and Media)
description:
The emergence of a highly competent bureaucracy is typically under appreciated, if not ignored, as an ingredient in the emergence of the American experiment. While government administrators are often derided as inefficient or even obstructionist, technocrats are responsible for making government work and offer policy continuity and expertise across administrations. Still, the growth of a large permanent bureaucracy challenges the notion of accountability grounded in democratic elections. By tracing historical developments and contemporary debates, this lecture focuses on the centrality of government administrative capacity in the American experiment, focusing on the tensions and collaboration between elected officials and professional bureaucrats. We will consider the role of independent government agencies that were established with the idea of partitioning regulatory functions from partisan pressure.

Hosted by: Campus Activities, Civic & Voter Engagement

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12050870

title: A History of the Cherry Orchards of Branch Brook Park
description:
This is a free Adult School class. Registration is required. Click here to register.

While the famous Cherry Blossom Trees have graced Branch Brook Park since 1927, an official festival took another 50 years.  First celebrated in 1976, the Cherry Blossom Festival in Newark's Branch Brook Park has become an annual event, bringing hundreds of thousands of visitors to the Park. 

During this talk, we'll discuss why there are Cherry Blossom Trees in Branch Brook Park, the meaning behind them, and the history of the Festivals that have taken place.

Find out about their origins in 1927 and the creation of the Cherry Blossom Festival in Branch Brook Park from Maressa MacFarlane, archivist of the Essex County Park System.

title: Mindfulness in the Garden - Early Spring Series
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekday Series) 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Tuesdays, 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Early Spring Session: Mar 24, Apr 7, 14, & 28 (No class 4/21) 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. This class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Winter session will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee:  Each 4-class Series: $80 Members / $100 General Public 
Series registration only. Pre Registration is required – there are no drop-ins for this class. 
 
Late Spring Session: May 5, 12 19 & 26 
Early Summer Session: June 2, 9, 16 & 23
