title: NJ Code & Coffee meetup - April
description:
NJ Code & Coffee
Calling all New Jersey techies to the **NJ Code and Coffee** tech community meetup at the EqualSpace ES550 Tech & Innovation campus!

Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal networking/co-working session for those in tech (and the tech-curious).
That includes operators, founders, and investors. Developers, data engineers, UX/UI designers, data scientists, product managers, software engineers, solution architects... **all are welcome!**

People of all skill and experience levels can attend, we love it that way. Many people (optionally) bring projects to work on, and many other people (optionally) socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you!

Come grab a cup of coffee and meet new people, share stories, find collaborators, talk about everything tech, talk about everything NOT tech, take a break from job applications, get away from the computer screen, make connections, make conversation, **make friends!**

**Agenda:**
**2pm:** Doors open, networking starts
**2:30pm:** Intro circle
**3pm:** networking continues
**5pm:** event ends

**The Intro Circle**
**Near the beginning of the event (about 2:30 pm), we will do a standup in a circle:**
We first talk about organizer announcements, updates, and logistics. Then we go around the circle where each person says:

* your name and job title/ role in tech/ tech stack,
* What you can help others with,
* What you would like help with,
* Job opportunities you're hiring for OR announce that you are looking for one. If none, that's cool.

After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized.

**Community Discord - come hang out before and after!:**
[www.codeandcoffee.chat](www.codeandcoffee.chat)

**The Venue**
Equal Space is a premium share-space created with multicultural business owners in mind, =SPACE equips founders with resources, programming and a powerful network.

Headquartered in the City of Newark, their share-space provides resources for startups, coaching for tech companies, and a wealth of intellectuals to collaborate and build with. The next wave in enterprises is being born within their walls. Become a part of the story: [EqualSpace.co/](https://equalspace.co/)

title: Croton Aqueduct Bike Trip
description:
Get ready for a scenic spin through history as we ride the legendary Old Croton Aqueduct! This unforgettable bike trip takes you along one of New York’s most beautiful and peaceful trails, where tree-lined paths, and charming river views, create the perfect backdrop for a day outdoors. As we cruise along the trail you’ll enjoy plenty of photo-worthy stops along the way. Expect a relaxed pace with time to explore, snack, and soak in the scenery. Whether you're in it for the fresh air, the history, or just a joyful ride with great company, this Croton Aqueduct adventure is a perfect blend of nature, culture, and fun on two wheels! 
How do I sign-up?

Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students - $10 | Faculty/Staff - $20

When and Where?

Sunday, April 12, 2026
10:00 am - 5:00 pm
Croton Aqueduct, NJ - Begins and ends at Student Recreation Center

What to bring:

Money for spending, lunch, water, comfortable shoes, sunscreen.

Potential Hazards: Hazardous weather, poisonous plants/allergies, insect bites, unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, and injuries, including sprains/strains, abrasions, lacerations and fractures.
 

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

description:
Green Team Ministry’s December Meeting will meet VIRTUALLY Wednesday, Dec. 10 at 7pm.
(Usually meets 2nd Sundays) 12-1pm in the FCC Library
Be part of planning events, celebrating Creation,  and living your faith by taking action for the planet. Email greenteam@fccmtc.com for Zoom link.
 
 
Green Team/Creation Justice Ministry monthly meetings: 2nd Sunday of the month, after worship, in the FCC library.  Our congregation is committed to the idea that creation care is central to each aspect of our church: our theology and worship, institutional life and practices, and our partnership and work in the wider community. Learn about FCC’s climate justice initiatives, be part of planning our events, and live your faith by taking action for the planet. 
Check out our Green Team Ministry page here: https://fccmontclair.org/green-team-ministry/
and read our Creation Justice Covenant here: https://www.fccmontclair.org/creation-justice-church/

title: Second Sunday Concert: Teatro Nuovo
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
April 12, 2PM 
Led by conductor/musicologist Will Crutchfield, Teatro Nuovo – Italian for “new theater” – is a young organization devoted to cutting-edge interpretation of classic Italian opera.  Since its debut in 2018, Teatro Nuovo has presented thirteen operas by Rossini, Donizetti, Bellini, Verdi, Ricci, and Mayr, culminating in the modern premiere of Carolina Uccelli’s Anna di Resburgo, which made the New York Times list of the 20 best Classical events for 2024.  This year Teatro Nuovo will celebrate the bicentennial of Italian opera in America.
Details and registration will be posted in March.

Explore the beauty of the Van Vleck Gardens this spring through reflective writing and creative mark-making.  This mindfulness-based class/series invites you to slow down, observe the natural world and turn your blank pages into a wonderland of personal observations.  Each class takes place in a different area of the Gardens (weather permitting), offering new inspiration for discovery.  Deepen your connection with nature and yourself in a no-pressure environment.  No art or writing experience necessary – just an openness to explore, reflect and create.   
Fee: Series Rate (4 classes): $50 Members / $70 General Public 
Individual Rate: $15 Members / $20 General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to class.

Tuesdays, 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Early Spring Session: Mar 24, Apr 7, 14, & 28 (No class 4/21) 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. This class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. title: Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair
description:
**Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair**  
  
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Join baseball historian Sam Bernstein for a lively survey of Jewish involvement in America’s National Pastime.  
  
Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.  
https://shomrei.easyshul.com/events/6967e303368f110026c0c410  
  
**\***Members & PreSchool Families should use their fob to access the building.  
  
**About the Program**  
Using audio and video, *Jews and Baseball* explores the origins of the game and the impact Jewish players, owners, and innovators have had on its evolution. The program highlights figures such as Hank Greenberg, Sandy Koufax, Barney Dreyfuss, Bud Selig, Lou Boudreau, Marvin Miller, as well as the game's current Jewish stars. It also delves into topics including antisemitism in the sport, baseball’s growth in Israel, and the lore of the “Koufax Curse.”  
  
**About Sam Bernstein**  
Sam Bernstein is a retired Social Worker who has published many articles on baseball history. He received a BA in History from the American University and an MSW from Adelphi University. After a career serving the Jewish community at JCCs and Jewish Family Service, Sam worked for the Elizabeth Public Schools and as an Instructor in Social Work for Rutgers University. He was raised in Malverne, NY and moved to New Jersey with his wife Maureen in 1999.  
  
Full_Season_Schedule_for_Midday_MeetUps: https://shomrei.org/calendar/midday-meetup-calendar  
  
**Questions?** Contact June Zimmerman,   
VP for Programming  
jzimmerman@shomrei.org  
  
\#middaymeetup

title: Awakening from Grief
description:
Awakening from Grief: Finding the Way back to Joy 
April 7th & 14th, 7:00 PM – 9:30 PM 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Van Vleck House is pleased to present AWAKENING FROM GRIEF: Finding the Way Back to Joy – one of our most popular workshops. Led by John E. Welshons, and based on his critically acclaimed book of the same title, AWAKENING FROM GRIEF offers the opportunity to explore – and heal – the psychological, emotional and spiritual challenges that follow in the wake of a loss or unexpected change. This workshop helps participants to discover the inner resources that will aid them in working through life’s inevitable challenges. We will gather in the living room of the Van Vleck Main House. Our time together will combine lecture, discussion, and a variety of meditative exercises. Participants complete the workshop feeling a profound sense of relief . . . and hope. Reading Awakening From Grief prior to the class is recommended, though not a requirement. 
Fee: Series (includes 2 classes) $65 for Members $80 for General Public 
Pre Registration is required by Monday, April 6th.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Virtual Meditation
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Virtual Meditation 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Wednesdays, 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM 
January 7, 14, 21, 28 
February 4, 11, 25 
March 4, 18, 25 
April 1, 8, 15, 22, 29 
Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. Open to beginners and experienced practitioners, these gatherings offer an opportunity to experience deep inner peace while practicing with a highly experienced meditation teacher.  
Important Updated Registration Information: 
A Zoom Link will be sent the day of the class. If you are registering after 3 PM on the day of the class, the Zoom link will be included in the automated payment confirmation email sent when you register. There is no registration deadline for this class. 
Fee: Single class: $10 Members / $15 General Public

title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'
For more information

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekend Classes)
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekend Classes) 
Saturdays, 9:30 AM – 10:30 AM 
March 28th 
April 18th 
May 9th 
June 13th 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. Each class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Cold or rainy weather – class will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee per class:  $20 Members / $25 General Public 
Pre Registration is required.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: EARTH WEEK CELEBRATION 2026
description:
The Northeast Earth Coalition (NEEC) invites you to join the 2026 Earth Week Celebration April 18-26, for a week of events on the theme of “OUR POWER OUR PLANET.” Between Saturday April 18 and Sunday April 26, we will have volunteer opportunities for cleanups in parks, public spaces, and community gardens. 
The Northeast Earth Coalition is partnering with the Township of Montclair, the Parks and Recreation Committee, Friends of Montclair Parks, and The Brookdale Park Conservancy. 
 
  
THE NEEC IS PART OF THE GREAT GLOBAL CLEANUP® MOVEMENT!
MONTCLAIR EARTH DAY EVENTS: 
APRIL 18, 10-noon:  

Open House at Pine Street Community Garden – come check out the garden and learn about the new solar-powered Rocket composter

APRIL 22, 7-9 pm: 

Movie screening: “The Little Things That Run the World” – part of the Bee City USA series.
Guild Room, First Congregational Church, Montclair

APRIL 25, 10-11:30 am: 

Crane Park Pollinator Garden clean up – help us get the garden ready for Spring, and see what’s already growing! 

APRIL 26, 12-4 pm:

Montclair Earth Fest
South Park St. between Bloomfield Ave. and Church St.


 
For additional information & volunteer opportunities, please e-mail us at: Info@neearth.org or check our calendar for other events and workshops. 

Make every day Earth Day

Fight climate change with diet change
Plant native plants
Stand against deforestation
Organize community cleanups
Compost
Reduce plastic use
Go pesticide free
Turn off lights when not in use
Save paper – switch to online billing
Support organic agriculture

title: Underground Church Worship Service,10:30am on the Labyrinth in the Sanctuary
description: Underground Church (usually every 3rd Saturday) is a unique and dynamic gathering that meets monthly, and combines the intimacy of Jesus' early community with creative and inventive worship experiences.  In the warmth and safety of community we explore the scriptures, live out the questions through discussion,  and share innovative rituals that bring us closer to each other and to our still-speaking God. Together we create the unity of spirit that helps us find God's presence in the messy and beautiful experience of living today.  All are welcome.

title: Roller Skating Hang Out - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
Join us for an hour or two of roller skating indoors at the Florham Park Roller Rink, 192 Ridgedale Ave. No experience is needed. All you have to do is show up! Come anytime. Open skate is tentatively scheduled from 1 p.m. until 3 p.m.

The cost is $16 per person, unless you happen to have your own skates. Then, it's $10! You walk inside, pay at the window on your own, and then you'll see a growing group of people around one of the first sets of tables likely between 12:50 and 1:20 p.m. Some parking is available on site.

If looking to learn more, here's the website link for the roller rink -> www.myrink.com.

\*\*\* We will likely hang out after skating at the Florham Park Diner, 182 Ridgedale Ave. But feel free to post ideas below in the comments section. Last time, we went to a place called Recess, a bar with pool tables, shuffleboard, a dart board and video games in Parsippany, thanks to a suggestion from Nigel. \*\*\*

Questions? Suggestions? Concerns? You can message me on Meetup or WhatsApp, or call/text me at 201-234-9609. Or feel free to comment below.

This event idea comes to you courtesy of Joe, one of our many regulars. Hope you'll consider attending our 7th skating event, or joining us for a bowling, volleyball, tennis or other event -> www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181

title: Shomrei Gives Back - Mitzvah Day
description:
**2nd Annual Mitzvah Day**  
  
Gather as a community in service to perform mitzvot for our local social action partners, Israel, and Shomrei itself. All ages welcome! There is a Mitzvah for everyone, including kids! Babysitting available. Breakfast nosh provided.  
  
Open to Shomrei Community & Friends.  
  
**Participate in any or all activities:**   
  
  
**DONATE SUPPLIES** Donate items needed to make our Mitzvot a reality. Visit our online wish list to make a purchase. Make sure items are delivered to Shomrei **by April 8.** Drop-offs at Shomrei are welcome. Or, fund the supplies with a monetary donation. You don't have to donate items to participate (but it helps!)  
**online wish list: https://shomrei.org/wishlist**  
**alternative monetary donation:** **https://shomrei.org/supplies**  
 **VOLUNTEER YOUR TIME**  
Choose your task when you arrive, among:  
- Assemble Senior grocery bags for Toni's Kitchen
- Sort donated spring & summer clothes for Human Needs Food Pantry
- Assemble "Welcome to Your New Home" kits for Family Promise
- Make sandwiches for MESH
- Garden outdoors at Shomrei
- Make friendship bracelets for a school in Israel (kid-specific)
- Color pictures/write letters for seniors (kid-specific)
- Write thank you cards for pet adoptions for the Montclair Animal Shelter (kid-specific)

  
**register here: https://shomrei.org/mitzvah  
  
**PARTICIPATE IN A DRIVE**  
Bring breakfast cereals and/or adult and children’s summer clothing for Human Needs Food Pantry to Shomrei (Drop off at Shomrei or bring with you on Mitzvah day.)  
  
**Questions?** Contact Melissa Mehl,   
Operations Manager  
mmehl@shomrei.org

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Movie screening: "The Little Things That Run the World" - Earth Week Celebration
location: Guild Room at First Congregational Church, 40 South Fullerton Avenue, Montclair, New Jersey, 07042, 07042, United States
description:
Join us for a screening of “The Little Things That Run the World,” part of our Bee City USA program. 
Synopsis:
Life on Earth is in the middle of a major re-alignment of species composition. The smallest of creatures – insects – hold the fabric of all life together. They are the most numerous of all animal groups on the planet. But insects are declining at an alarming rate throughout the world, threatening innumerable species, whole ecosystems, and even our food systems with collapse. This is story about untangling the mysteries as to why something so ubiquitous – and seemingly endless – could be on the cusp of disappearing entirely. It is also the story of heroic efforts to save these most vulnerable of creatures. 
Admission is free. Register to save your seat. 
Learn more about the film here.

title: Here All Along: Sarah Hurwitz on Finding Judaism
description:
**Seth Goodchild Memorial Lecture**  
Reception immediately following  
  
RSVP:   
  
**About the Program**  
During the final years of her tenure in the White House as First Lady Michelle Obama’s head speechwriter, Sarah reconnected with Judaism and was astonished to discover thousands of years of wisdom from her ancestors about what it means to be human. She found beautiful rituals that honor the past and sanctify the present; wildly diverse conceptions of God, none of which were a bearded man in the sky; and guidance on living an ethical life that set a much higher bar for honesty, generosity, and basic human decency than she had ever thought to set for herself. Sarah shares insights about how the wisdom of an ancient tradition can transform our lives today, inspiring Jews to embrace and celebrate their heritage.   
  
**Free Babysitting Available**  
Email Aviva Arad, aarad@shomrei.org  
  
**\*Additional parking is available next door at 73 & 77 Park Street.**  
  
**In Loving Memory of Seth Goodchild**   
Seth Goodchild was inspired by trips to Israel to convert to Judaism at age 61. To honor his life and legacy, his friends and family established a fund for Shomrei Emunah to continue and spread his commitment to Judaism for the broader community.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
**Join Us for the Fourth Annual Fairy Trail Day!**

The fairies of the South Mountain Reservation invite you to our **4th Annual Fairy Tales on the Fairy Trail**! This festival-style event promises a day filled with fun and magic, including: storytelling, fairy house building, fairy hair, a magical scavenger hunt and more!

Wings, crowns, and t-shirts will be available for purchase, with all proceeds benefiting the South Mountain Reservation Fairy Trail.

Dress up in your sparkliest attire, complete with crowns and wings (or purchase them at the event), and let’s celebrate the magic of the Fairy Trail together!

All proceeds go directly to the SMC Fairy Trail Fund.

title: NJ Real Estate Broker 90-Hour Basic Broker Course | Burgos Realty Academy
description: Registration Open NJ Real Estate Broker 90-Hour Basic Broker Course (Evening/Weekend) May 7th - June 18th T/W/Th/Sat Burgos Realty Academy offers the 90-hour Basic Broker course as part of New Jersey’s required 150-hour Broker pre-licensing curriculum. This instructor-led course covers advanced real estate law, brokerage practices, contracts, finance, valuation, and regulatory requirements essential for broker licensure and long-term success. The full 150-hour New Jersey Broker pre-licensing requirement includes: 30 Hours – Ethics & Agency 30 Hours – Office Management 90 Hours – Basic Broker Courses are taught by a licensed broker-owner, real estate instructor, and ARELLO Certified Distance Education Instructor. Instruction is designed to prepare students not only to pass the broker exam, but to operate with confidence, compliance, and professional authority in today’s real estate industry.

title: Noontime Cornhole - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
This is an event for two to three hours of casual cornhole at a local park.

The gathering is tentatively scheduled for Lodi Memorial Park, behind the library, 1 Memorial Dr., because the area has two permanent concrete boards.

There is not a ton of parking, but a huge lot across the street as part of the shopping plaza.

Comment below if you have your own boards and beanbags, or if you want to bring another yard game. A volleyball court and basketball court, as well as turf field, are also on site. If anyone knows of any other permanent cornhole setups in North Jersey, feel free to also make that suggestion. We may relocate as a result.

\*\*\* After playing, we'll likely be heading somewhere for a drink and snack. Feel free to suggest a spot for a post-cornhole hangout. \*\*\*

There's no fee to play and nothing else you need to do besides show up. Come anytime!! All are welcome!!! We're not in a league and are not here to analyze your form. We're here for people who haven't played in months, maybe years, and just want to occasionally play and hang out.

The event comes to you courtesy of prior suggestions for future events and our group's WhatsApp chat.

Hope you'll join us for our first cornhole event. Questions or comments? You can message Andy on Meetup, or call/text him at 201-234-9609.
Check out bowling, soccer, volleyball, tennis, skating or one of our other activities at: [www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball](https://www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball)
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

(Usually meets 2nd Sundays) 12-1pm in the FCC Library
Be part of planning events, celebrating Creation,  and living your faith by taking action for the planet. Email greenteam@fccmtc.com for Zoom link.
 
 
Green Team/Creation Justice Ministry monthly meetings: 2nd Sunday of the month, after worship, in the FCC library.  Our congregation is committed to the idea that creation care is central to each aspect of our church: our theology and worship, institutional life and practices, and our partnership and work in the wider community. Learn about FCC’s climate justice initiatives, be part of planning our events, and live your faith by taking action for the planet. 
Check out our Green Team Ministry page here: https://fccmontclair.org/green-team-ministry/
and read our Creation Justice Covenant here: https://www.fccmontclair.org/creation-justice-church/

title: Midday MeetUp - Jersey Tomato
description:
**Midday Meetup - Jersey Tomato  
  
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Food historian, Judith Krall-Russo, will talk about where the tomato originated and why it became one of New Jersey’s favorite crops, the history of the tomato industry in New Jersey, about heirloom varieties, modern hybrids and the folklore attached to tomatoes.  
  
**Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: SMR Mayapple Trail Runs
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Runners are invited to register for the 17th annual Mayapple Trail Runs to be held on Saturday, May 16, 2026 in beautiful South Mountain Reservation in Essex County, N.J. based out of the Locust Grove Picnic Area, 197 Glen Avenue, Millburn.

The races are organized by the volunteers of the South Mountain Conservancy (SMC) with the support of the Essex County Parks Department.
The USA Track and Field sanctioned races provide an informal but supportive trail race environment for runners who are up to the challenge of completing 5, 10, or 20 miles, or ultra-marathoon distances of 50K (31 miles) and 50 miles. There is also an informal, untimed one-mile race for kids 14 and under. Starting times depend on the race. The 50k and 50 mile races start at 8:30. All proceeds support the mission of the Conservancy as a steward and advocate for the park and its public programs.

## Millburn, NJ • 50 Miler, 50K, 20 Miler, 10 Miler, 5 Miler, Kids 1 Mile Run

**Registration closes: Sat, May 16 @ 11:59PM ET**
**To register as a runner or volunteer (we need you!) go to [Ultrasignup.](https://ultrasignup.com/register.aspx?did=135418) For very detailed descriptions and information on group discounts, go to our MTR [website](https://sites.google.com/view/south-mtn-mayapple-trail-runs).**

title: Last Day Of RE (All Ages Hike)
Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

Members from Bnai Keshet, Temple Sholom of West Essex, Temple Ner Tamid, and Congregation Shomrei Emunah will once again come together to study and discuss interesting Jewish topics late into the night. title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'
For more information

title: Writing as Spiritual Practice Covenant Group
description:
Are you interested in exploring the monthly themes through writing and sharing with others? The purpose of this group is to write about the monthly theme before the meeting and share your writing with the group. Facilitated by Dennis Kurtti, it will be held each month on the fourth Saturday. This is not a writing critique group, and all levels of writing experience are welcome. If you would like to understand more about this kind of covenant group, you can watch the worship service held on 8/21/22, which featured speakers from an already existing group. To express your interest in this group, contact Laura Wilson at CovenantGroups@uumontclair.org

title: Montclair Garden Tour 2026
location: 69 Grove Street, Montclair, NJ 07042, 69 Grove Street, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
OPEN GARDEN TOUR
MAY 23, 2:30- 5:30PM at 69 Grove Street, Montclair 
Come and see a beautiful, ecofriendly home garden featuring: 

A NWF Certified Wildlife Habitat & Monarch Waystation
25 years of landscaping with native plants
A sanctuary for birds and pollinators
A highly productive organic vegetable garden
A backyard pond
A solar-powered home
Over 170 species of native plants
A mature micro-forest
Container gardening
The highest recorded number of native bee species in a single site in Essex County (According to a 2015 survey by the NY Natural History Museum)

See what was done in one yard – Find inspiration for your own yard!

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Connection Cafe- In person
description: You are invited to join us for our in person Connection Café every Sunday at 11:00 AM. Coffee will be provided. If you haven’t yet experienced our online Connection Café, give it a try. Participants report that this format allows us to build relationships across the congregation in ways that will sustain us as individual participants and support our growth as a community. So grab your coffee and join us for a fun way to connect on Sunday morning. All are welcome. We hope you can join us!

title: Newcomer Connections
description:
Register: https://tinyurl.com/UUCMNC2526
  • New to the Unitarian Universalist Congregation at Montclair? 
  • Visiting or "Congregation Shopping" for yourself or your family? 
  • Want to know a bit more about our congregation and the Unitarian Universalist tradition?

Join with one of our ministers and other newcomers to for a short, honest, in-depth conversation. All of your questions will be honored. Children age 12 and above are welcome to participate.

title: Midday MeetUp - Bonnie Rapp, Best of Broadway
description:
**Midday MeetUp - Bonnie Rapp, Best of Broadway   
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Experience *The Best of Broadway* as Bonnie Rapp brings to life unforgettable moments from Tony Award–winning musicals. Relive the golden age of musical theatre through beloved classics and timeless melodies by legendary composers such as Rodgers and Hammerstein, Lerner and Loewe, Frank Loesser, Irving Berlin and more!  
  
Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.  
https://shomrei.easyshul.com/events/6967e6f2368f110026c0c486  
  
**\***Members & PreSchool Families should use their fob to access the building.  
 About Bonnie Rapp**  
Bonnie had the honor of performing the lead role of Christine Daaé in the National Tour/San Francisco Company of Andrew Lloyd Webber's The Phantom of the Opera. Some of Bonnie’s favorite roles include Maria in The Sound of Music, Kate in Kiss Me, Kate, Cinderella in Rodgers and Hammerstein’s Cinderella, Louise in Gypsy and Sister Amnesia and Sister Leo in Nunsense, Nunsense II and Nunsense Jamboree. In addition, Bonnie has appeared in numerous galas, fundraisers, and Broadway concerts, including performances at Lincoln Center's Avery Fisher Hall.  
  
For the past decade, Bonnie has devoted her talents to providing live musical entertainment for a wide range of audiences, including corporate and community events, libraries, private celebrations, JCCs, and residents of independent living, assisted living, nursing homes, and memory care communities.  
  
Full_Season_Schedule_for_Midday_MeetUps: https://shomrei.org/calendar/midday-meetup-calendar  
  
**Questions?** Contact June Zimmerman,   
VP for Programming  
jzimmerman@shomrei.org  
  
\#middaymeetup

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Chalice Choir Rehearsal
description:
The Chalice Choir is open to anyone who enjoys singing. No need to audition, and reading music is not a requirement. The choir sings a wide repertoire of music, from Handel to Nina Simone, Leonard Cohen and Bobby McFerrin. Opportunities to solo or sing in small groups are often available for more confident singers.

Regular rehearsals are on Wednesday evenings from 7:30 to 9:00 PM in the Sanctuary. For more information contact Markus.

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181
